prose:
**Benzaldehyde Fukui Reactivity (DFT B3LYP/def2-TZVP, gas phase)**

The fukui-reactivity skill was executed using DFT with the B3LYP functional and def2-TZVP basis set (tier standard, density-fitted) via PySCF, computing Fukui indices on the neutral molecule and its N±1 electronic states (cation and anion, both computed at multiplicity 2 to properly treat open-shell systems). The geometry was taken as-is from the input .xyz file without re-optimization at DFT level.

**Most electrophilic atom:** C atom 3 (ortho position), f⁺ = 0.39938968
**Most nucleophilic atom:** O atom 1 (carbonyl oxygen), f⁻ = 0.23839906

All three SCF calculations converged cleanly. Mulliken partial charges were used. Fukui charge conservation was verified (sum f⁺ = 1.0, sum f⁻ = 1.0). The dual descriptor and per-atom Fukui indices (f⁺, f⁻, f⁰) are tabulated above. A reactivity visualization was produced at `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/benzaldehyde/argo_claude-haiku-4.5/20260702-201229_benzaldehyde_fukui_reactivity/agent_call_02_plot.png`. The live log streamed to `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/benzaldehyde/argo_claude-haiku-4.5/20260702-201229_benzaldehyde_fukui_reactivity/fukui_20260702-201237.out`.
